summary: Gruber shares a quip from Adam Engst about the O'Reilly Mac OS X Conference, where four or five attendees identified as former NeXT users. The humor lies in Engst's implication that four or five people constitutes 'a large percentage of the NeXT user population,' poking fun at how small NeXT's market share was. It's a brief link post that uses someone else's joke to make a point about NeXT's niche status and its outsized influence on Mac OS X.
key_points: NeXT had a tiny user base despite its technical significance
Mac OS X's Unix underpinnings drew former NeXT users to the platform
The joke highlights the irony that NeXT's technology became the foundation of Apple's future despite negligible market penetration
money_quotes: Four or five people even said they'd been NeXT users — it was nice to see such a large percentage of the NeXT user population represented at the conference.
key_insight: NeXT's minuscule user base belied its enormous technical influence on what became Mac OS X.
